title: CNN This Morning · 2026-06-11 · 美伊冲突升级
summary: 美国与伊朗连续第二天互相打击，特朗普威胁若伊朗不立即同意和平协议将加大轰炸。国防部长赫格塞斯称将用炸弹谈判。伊朗大使表示不会在威胁下谈判。前国防情报官员曼恩指出轰炸无效，并提及美国可能对伊朗民用基础设施发动袭击，构成战争罪。此外，尼克斯队上演29分大逆转，赢得NBA总决赛第4场。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Anchor
paragraphs.en: Well, that does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Brad Smith. CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: 好了，CNN头条快讯到此结束。我是布拉德·史密斯。奥蒂·科尼什主持的《CNN今晨》节目现在开始。

paragraphs.speaker: Erica Hill
paragraphs.en: New overnight, the United States and Iran trading attacks for a second straight day as President Trump promises more to come if Tehran does not immediately agree to a peace deal.
paragraphs.cn: 昨夜最新消息，美国和伊朗连续第二天互相攻击，特朗普总统承诺，如果德黑兰不立即同意和平协议，还会有更多行动。

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.en: We're going to be attacking them and attacking them very hard, and we'll see what happens with the deal.
paragraphs.cn: 我们将对他们进行非常猛烈的攻击，看看协议会有什么结果。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Plus, new revelations about how the White House handled the Epstein scandal, meeting in the Situation Room to game out a plan without President Trump.
paragraphs.cn: 此外，关于白宫如何处理爱泼斯坦丑闻的新爆料：在战情室开会，在没有特朗普总统参与的情况下制定计划。

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.en: You know what I really love? I love the inflation.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道我真正喜欢什么吗？我喜欢通货膨胀。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: The president suggests, as you just heard, he doesn't mind the high prices right now. But what about Americans paying more for groceries and gas? A stabbing in Northern Ireland sparking days of unrest. Police under attack. The streets in chaos.
paragraphs.cn: 正如你刚才听到的，总统暗示他并不介意目前的高物价。但美国人在食品杂货和汽油上花更多钱呢？北爱尔兰发生持刀伤人事件，引发数日骚乱。警察遭到袭击。街头一片混乱。

paragraphs.speaker: Gianni Infantino
paragraphs.en: The soul and the heart is the fans in the stadiums. It's the fans in the fan zones.
paragraphs.cn: 灵魂和核心是体育场里的球迷，是球迷区的球迷。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: The World Cup officially kicking off today, finally. Could we also see a much-needed vibe shift away from the outrage over ticket prices and immigration issues?
paragraphs.cn: 世界杯今天终于正式开幕。我们是否也能看到一种急需的氛围转变，摆脱对票价和移民问题的愤怒？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Shot no good. The tip-up's good. It's good, it's good. With one point.
paragraphs.cn: 投篮不中。补篮进了。进了，进了。一分。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: A historic comeback. Down 29 points. That last-second tip gives the Knicks a 3-1 advantage. Now just one win away from the NBA championship. (CHEERING)
paragraphs.cn: 历史性的逆转。落后29分。最后一秒的补篮让尼克斯队以3-1领先。现在距离NBA总冠军仅差一场胜利。（欢呼声）

paragraphs.speaker: Trump
paragraphs.en: We were really close to a deal, but they keep tapping us along. They keep playing us for suckers.
paragraphs.cn: 我们本来非常接近达成协议，但他们一直在拖延我们。他们一直把我们当傻子耍。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: President Trump choosing military might over diplomacy. But can he bomb Tehran into a deal? Good morning, everyone. I'm Erica Hill, in on this Thursday for Audie Cornish. Nice to have you with us. We do begin this hour with the tit-for-tat strikes between the U.S. and Iran and the promise for more from the White House. (EXPLOSIONS)
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普总统选择军事力量而非外交手段。但他能通过轰炸迫使德黑兰达成协议吗？大家早上好。我是埃里卡·希尔，在这个周四接替奥蒂·科尼什。很高兴有你们陪伴。我们本小时以美国和伊朗之间的针锋相对打击以及白宫承诺更多行动开始。（爆炸声）

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: The U.S. says it targeted Iran's surveillance capabilities, communication systems and air defense sites. Iran hitting back with attacks on U.S. bases in the region. On a call from the Situation Room, President Trump told FOX News if Iran did not agree to a deal, he would bomb the expletive out of them, calling this the most violated ceasefire in history.
paragraphs.cn: 美国表示其目标是伊朗的监视能力、通信系统和防空设施。伊朗则对该地区的美国基地发动攻击作为报复。在战情室的一次通话中，特朗普总统告诉福克斯新闻，如果伊朗不同意达成协议，他将对他们进行猛烈轰炸，并称这是历史上最被违反的停火协议。

paragraphs.speaker: Pete Hegseth
paragraphs.en: You can see when someone's trying to tap, tap, tap on a deal. Instead, they're going to have tap, tap, tap bombs dropping on key facilities in Iran from the United States of America. If we need to negotiate with bombs, we'll negotiate with bombs. And we're very good at it.
paragraphs.cn: 你可以看到当有人试图拖延协议时。相反，他们将看到来自美利坚合众国的炸弹不断落在伊朗的关键设施上。如果我们需要用炸弹谈判，我们就用炸弹谈判。而且我们非常擅长这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: So, will those threats actually get Tehran to the table?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，这些威胁真的能让德黑兰回到谈判桌吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Amir-Saeid Iravani
paragraphs.en: No sustainable deal can be reached through threats, intimidation, or the use of force. The president of the United States should refrain from his recurring threats against Iran, including his renewed threat of force made today. Iran has never negotiated under threats and pressure and will never submit to pressure or coercion.
paragraphs.cn: 通过威胁、恐吓或使用武力无法达成任何可持续的协议。美国总统应停止对伊朗的反复威胁，包括今天再次发出的武力威胁。伊朗从未在威胁和压力下进行谈判，也永远不会屈服于压力或胁迫。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Joining me now, former defense intelligence official, Major Harrison Mann. It's good to have you with us this morning. So, we heard from Secretary Hegseth there saying, you know, we will continue to negotiate with bombs. Is that actually the way to get Iran to the table? It does not seem to be working in this moment.
paragraphs.cn: 现在加入我们的是前国防情报官员哈里森·曼少校。今早很高兴有您加入。我们听到赫格塞斯部长说，我们将继续用炸弹谈判。这真的是让伊朗回到谈判桌的方法吗？目前看来似乎并不奏效。

paragraphs.speaker: Maj. Harrison Mann
paragraphs.en: Hi, Erica. We know for certain from recent history that it is not the way to get Iran to negotiate. They tried bombing Iran for 40 days. It didn't work. Then they tried the blockade for another two months. That hasn't worked. And they're out of ideas. So, I guess they're trying to bomb a second time. It's -- it's unfortunate, because I think there is still a possibility for peace when the Trump administration gets serious about actually negotiating, which is going to require them making some concessions.
paragraphs.cn: 你好，埃里卡。从近期历史我们可以肯定，这不是让伊朗谈判的方式。他们曾试图轰炸伊朗40天，没有奏效。然后他们又尝试封锁两个月，也没有成功。他们已经无计可施。所以，我想他们正试图再次轰炸。这很不幸，因为我认为当特朗普政府认真对待实际谈判时，和平仍有可能，但这需要他们做出一些让步。

paragraphs.speaker: Maj. Harrison Mann
paragraphs.en: But for now, they're clearly not willing to do that. And so, they're going to start bombing again. The -- the other really worrying escalation we saw yesterday was an apparent war crime: a U.S. targeted precision strike on a water reservoir for drinking water in Iran near Hormuz. And that's something Trump has threatened several times over the past eight weeks, you know, when he got frustrated with the ceasefire.
paragraphs.cn: 但目前，他们显然不愿意这样做。所以，他们将再次开始轰炸。昨天我们看到的另一个令人担忧的升级是明显的战争罪行：美国对伊朗霍尔木兹附近的一个饮用水水库进行了精准打击。而这是特朗普在过去八周内多次威胁过的，当他因停火感到沮丧时。

paragraphs.speaker: Maj. Harrison Mann
paragraphs.en: And I'm hoping we don't see more war crimes, targeting of civilian infrastructure, as a deliberate strategy to try and bring Iran to the table.
paragraphs.cn: 我希望我们不会看到更多战争罪行，不会将民用基础设施作为目标，作为试图迫使伊朗回到谈判桌的蓄意策略。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Certainly, there are a lot of --
paragraphs.cn: 当然，有很多 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Mann
paragraphs.en: It's illegal, and it won't work.
paragraphs.cn: 这是非法的，而且行不通。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: There are a lot of questions about that. And questions into CENTCOM for more clarity -- right? -- on what the exact targets were in terms of that, and whether that water plant was one of them. As we look at where things stand this morning, to your point about getting serious about the negotiations, we have heard so much from President Trump publicly.
paragraphs.cn: 关于这一点有很多疑问。需要向中央司令部寻求更多澄清 -- -- 对吧？ -- -- 关于具体目标是什么，以及那个水厂是否在其中之一。当我们今天早上审视局势时，就你提到的认真对待谈判这一点，我们听到了特朗普总统在公开场合的许多言论。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: You know, and CNN did an excellent analysis by our colleague Aaron Blake earlier this week about the number of times that President Trump has said, We're close, close to a deal. We've heard it in the last several days. There is so much public posturing from this president. Are you concerned at all that that could weaken the U.S. position?
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，CNN本周早些时候由我们的同事Aaron Blake做了一项出色的分析，关于特朗普总统说过多少次"我们接近达成协议了"。过去几天我们一直听到这种说法。这位总统有很多公开表态。你是否担心这会削弱美国的立场？

paragraphs.speaker: Mann
paragraphs.en: I think it already has weakened the U.S. position. What the Iranian side understood at the start of the war -- which, let's remember, began when Trump and Netanyahu bombed in the middle of negotiations -- is that it's very hard to trust Donald Trump. And Trump's constant lying. you know, both in public and maybe to the Iranian side, has only further undermined that trust.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为这已经削弱了美国的立场。伊朗方面在战争开始时 -- -- 让我们记住，战争始于特朗普和内塔尼亚胡在谈判期间进行轰炸 -- -- 就明白很难信任唐纳德·特朗普。而特朗普不断撒谎，无论是在公开场合还是可能对伊朗方面，都进一步破坏了这种信任。

paragraphs.speaker: Mann
paragraphs.en: So, when it looks like maybe they're making progress towards a deal, and then Trump comes out and leaks some of the details, or he just starts making really outrageous threats, that's going to make it even harder for him to earn the trust of his negotiating counterpart and get them to actually agree to something.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，当看起来他们可能在达成协议方面取得进展时，特朗普却出来泄露一些细节，或者开始发出非常离谱的威胁，这会让他更难赢得谈判对手的信任，并让他们真正同意某些事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Mann
paragraphs.en: And I think that's why we're seeing an increased willingness from the Iranian government to go back to full-scale war, because they don't really think at this point that there's any other way to convince Trump to negotiate in good faith.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为这就是为什么我们看到伊朗政府越来越愿意回到全面战争，因为他们现在真的认为没有其他办法可以说服特朗普真诚谈判。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: When this war began -- of course, it was the U.S. and Israel when this war began. When it comes to Israel, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu put out this statement yesterday, this message, I should say, for the people of Lebanon. I want to play that for you.
paragraphs.cn: 当这场战争开始时 -- -- 当然，是美国和以色列发起的。就以色列而言，总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡昨天发表了一份声明，我应该说，是给黎巴嫩人民的信息。我想为你播放一下。

paragraphs.speaker: Benjamin Netanyahu
paragraphs.en: I have a message for you, the people of Lebanon. Israel is not at war with you. We're at war with Hezbollah that has taken your country hostage, that does Iran's bidding, that uses your territory to launch terrorist attacks against Israel. Do you remember what Lebanon was like before Iran and Hezbollah turned it into a nightmare? Once Hezbollah is dismantled, the possibilities are endless, and they are sky-high.
paragraphs.cn: 我有一句话要对你们黎巴嫩人民说。以色列不是在和你们作战。我们是在与真主党作战，他们劫持了你们的国家，为伊朗效力，利用你们的领土对以色列发动恐怖袭击。你们还记得在伊朗和真主党把黎巴嫩变成噩梦之前是什么样子吗？一旦真主党被瓦解，可能性是无限的，而且前景光明。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: That's notable, of course, coming on the heels of Iran and Israel trading -- trading strikes earlier in the week, specifically on Sunday. On Sunday, too, in the wake of Iran's missiles, President Trump told "The Financial Times" in an interview that Israel is going to have to accept whatever deal the U.S. makes with Iran and that Israel doesn't call the shots.
paragraphs.cn: 这当然值得注意，因为此前伊朗和以色列在本周早些时候 -- -- 具体是周日 -- -- 互相发动了袭击。同样在周日，伊朗导弹袭击后，特朗普总统在接受《金融时报》采访时表示，以色列将不得不接受美国与伊朗达成的任何协议，以色列说了不算。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Based on what we're seeing, how much sway, how much influence do you think this president actually has when it comes to Prime Minister Netanyahu and the decisions that Israel is making?
paragraphs.cn: 根据我们目前看到的情况，你认为这位总统对内塔尼亚胡总理以及以色列正在做出的决定实际上有多大影响力？

paragraphs.speaker: Mann
paragraphs.en: So, a reminder that President Trump and any U.S. president has nearly infinite leverage over the Israeli government. Essentially, their air force can't fly without our support. You know, it's not just bombs and aircraft and reply [SIC] parts -- supply parts. You know, we have U.S. troops on Israeli soil, defending the skies of Israel, so those planes can safely land. We supply the fuel that goes into those aircraft.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，提醒一下，特朗普总统和任何一位美国总统对以色列政府几乎拥有无限的杠杆作用。基本上，没有我们的支持，他们的空军就无法飞行。你知道，这不仅仅是炸弹、飞机和备件 -- -- 供应零件。我们还有美军驻扎在以色列领土上，保卫以色列的领空，这样那些飞机才能安全降落。我们提供这些飞机所需的燃料。

paragraphs.speaker: Mann
paragraphs.en: So, if Trump wanted to use it, I'm pretty confident that he could end any Israeli military aggression that he wanted to. And we can see that he wants to do that a little bit, but clearly, not enough that it's deterred Netanyahu from launching what potentially started this escalation, which was an Israeli strike on Beirut on Sunday. So, Trump is still kind of trying to end the war with half measures. And it's clear that that's not going to work.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，如果特朗普想利用这一点，我相当确信他可以结束任何他想要结束的以色列军事侵略。我们可以看到他想稍微这样做，但显然，这还不足以阻止内塔尼亚胡发动可能引发这次升级的行动，也就是周日以色列对贝鲁特的袭击。所以，特朗普仍然在试图用半吊子的措施结束战争。很明显，这行不通。

paragraphs.speaker: Mann
paragraphs.en: He's going to have to do some difficult things, including actually putting his foot down with Netanyahu, as well as possibly making some concessions to the Iranian government. And unfortunately, I don't think he feels the urgency to do either of those things, because they can both be politically costly. And he's not going to feel that urgency until maybe we've seen another round of full-scale war that's totally unnecessary.
paragraphs.cn: 他将不得不做一些困难的事情，包括真正对内塔尼亚胡采取强硬立场，以及可能向伊朗政府做出一些让步。不幸的是，我认为他并不觉得有紧迫感去做这两件事中的任何一件，因为这两件事都可能付出政治代价。他可能要到我们再次看到一轮完全不必要的全面战争时才会感到紧迫。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Major Harrison Mann, really appreciate your insight this morning. Thank you.
paragraphs.cn: 哈里森·曼少校，非常感谢您今天早上的见解。谢谢。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Coming up here on CNN THIS MORNING, President Trump doubling down on his temporary pick for national intelligence director, a man with questionable qualifications, including a lack of actual intel experience. And Social Security at risk. New estimates on when the funding will run out. And the bleak warning signs for younger workers. Plus, a shot for the ages. Knicks fans celebrating that last-second win in game four.
paragraphs.cn: 接下来在CNN今晨节目中，特朗普总统坚持他临时选定的国家情报总监人选，此人资质存疑，包括缺乏实际情报经验。社会保障面临风险。关于资金何时耗尽的新估计。以及年轻工人的黯淡警示信号。此外，还有一记历史性的投篮。尼克斯球迷庆祝第四场比赛最后一秒的胜利。

paragraphs.speaker: O.G. Anunoby
paragraphs.en: It feels cool. I mean, everyone's pretty excited. I'm excited, too.
paragraphs.cn: 感觉很酷。我的意思是，每个人都很兴奋。我也很兴奋。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Thirteen minutes past the hour. Now, here's your morning roundup. Microsoft co-founder Bill Gates telling lawmakers on Wednesday he made a, quote, "huge mistake" in meeting with Jeffrey Epstein. He has denied any wrongdoing. Gates testified before the House Oversight Committee in a closed-door meeting, saying he was aware Epstein had been convicted of a horrific crime but continued to interact with him to seek money for his foundation.
paragraphs.cn: 现在是整点过13分。以下是您的早间新闻摘要。微软联合创始人比尔·盖茨周三向议员们表示，他与杰弗里·爱泼斯坦会面是一个"巨大的错误"。他否认有任何不当行为。盖茨在众议院监督委员会的闭门会议上作证，称他知道爱泼斯坦因骇人听闻的罪行被定罪，但为了为其基金会寻求资金而继续与他互动。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: When Gates arrived on Capitol Hill, he told reporters he would cooperate with any investigation.
paragraphs.cn: 当盖茨抵达国会山时，他告诉记者，他将配合任何调查。

paragraphs.speaker: Bill Gates
paragraphs.en: I'm glad to be here voluntarily to testify. I hope my testimony is helpful to the work, important work of the committee to find justice for the victims. Thank you.
paragraphs.cn: 我很高兴自愿来这里作证。我希望我的证词有助于委员会为受害者伸张正义的重要工作。谢谢。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Gates has not been accused in connection with Epstein's crimes. If you are counting on Social Security as part of your retirement plan, well, listen up. The latest government projections suggest the fund could run out of money in late 2032. So, that means the agency would no longer be able to pay 100 percent of benefits in the fourth quarter of that year. And in the years after, the monthly benefits will drastically decline. Some new details for you on that upcoming UFC fight at the White House.
paragraphs.cn: 盖茨并未被指控与爱泼斯坦的罪行有关。如果你指望社会保障作为退休计划的一部分，那么请听好。最新的政府预测显示，该基金可能在2032年底耗尽资金。这意味着，该机构将无法在当年第四季度支付100%的福利。而在之后的几年里，月度福利将大幅下降。关于即将在白宫举行的UFC比赛，有一些新细节要告诉你。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Specifically, the cost: some $60 million. Now, that's according to court documents submitted by the Trump administration, which says the money will cover logistics; things like food and porta-potties. The fight is set for Sunday night, which, of course, is also the president's birthday. Still to come here on CNN THIS MORNING, a record-setting win for the Knicks. Will Leitch is here with more on New York's 29-point comeback and the push on to game five.
paragraphs.cn: 具体来说，费用约为6000万美元。这是根据特朗普政府提交的法庭文件得出的，文件称这笔钱将用于后勤保障，比如食物和移动厕所。比赛定于周日晚上举行，当然，那天也是总统的生日。接下来在CNN今晨节目中，尼克斯队创纪录的胜利。威尔·莱奇将带来更多关于纽约队29分大逆转以及第五场比赛的报道。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Plus, the World Cup kicking off across America, North America today. Not without controversy. How about a live look for you here in Mexico City, the site of the opening match between Mexico and South Africa. So, can the action on the pitch finally offer a much-needed vibe shift?
paragraphs.cn: 此外，世界杯今天在北美各地开赛。并非没有争议。让我们在墨西哥城现场看一下，这里是墨西哥队与南非队揭幕战的举办地。那么，球场上的表现能否最终带来急需的氛围转变？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: I say, "New York," you say, "Yes!" New York!
paragraphs.cn: 我说"纽约"，你说"耶！"纽约！

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: A little excitement there on the subway. Frankly, everywhere in New York after the Knicks pulled off the greatest comeback in NBA finals history, securing a two-game lead over the Spurs.
paragraphs.cn: 地铁里有点兴奋。坦率地说，在尼克斯队完成NBA总决赛历史上最伟大的逆转、确保对马刺队两场领先优势后，纽约到处都这样。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: At the top, one on one.
paragraphs.cn: 开场，一对一。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: Brunson gets it, fires up a three. No good. The triplet's good! It's good! It's good with 1.20 left.
paragraphs.cn: 布伦森接球，投三分。没进。三分进了！进了！还剩1.20秒时进了。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: It was very good. The stunning end to a 29-point comeback. O.G. Anunoby's tip-in with 1.2 seconds remaining. The Knicks beating the Spurs by just one point, 107 to 106.
paragraphs.cn: 非常精彩。29分大逆转的惊人结局。OG·阿奴诺比在还剩1.2秒时补篮得手。尼克斯队以107比106仅一分之差击败马刺队。

paragraphs.speaker: Anunoby
paragraphs.en: Feels cool. I mean, everyone's pretty excited. I'm excited, too. I'm excited, too. We're all excited. We're enjoying it right now, but we're just focused on the next game now.
paragraphs.cn: 感觉太酷了。我的意思是，每个人都很兴奋。我也很兴奋。我也很兴奋。我们都很兴奋。我们现在正在享受这一刻，但我们只专注于下一场比赛。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Knicks fans celebrating, perhaps not surprisingly, into the wee hours of the morning. New York now has the opportunity to clinch its first NBA title since 1973. The next game, game five, Saturday night in San Antonio. Joining us now, Will Leitch. He's a contributing editor at "New York" magazine and a columnist with "The Washington Post." He's got his Knicks blue jacket on, his Jalen Brunson bobblehead somewhere. I mean, Knicks in five. There is -- I know we've talked a lot about this.
paragraphs.cn: 尼克斯球迷庆祝，也许并不意外，一直持续到凌晨。纽约现在有机会自1973年以来首次夺得NBA总冠军。下一场比赛，第五场，周六晚上在圣安东尼奥举行。现在加入我们的是威尔·莱奇。他是《纽约》杂志的特约编辑和《华盛顿邮报》的专栏作家。他穿着尼克斯队的蓝色夹克，他的杰伦·布伦森摇头娃娃放在某个地方。我的意思是，尼克斯五场解决。我知道我们已经讨论了很多。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: I have to say being in New York City. And it's true. You're on the subway. Everywhere you go, the excitement and the camaraderie, and the way this city has come together. It is something. And that was a hell of a game. WILL LEITCH, CONTRIBUTING EDITOR, "NEW YORK"
paragraphs.cn: 我不得不说，身处纽约市，这是真的。无论你走到哪里，地铁上，到处都是兴奋和友情，以及这座城市团结一致的方式。这真是了不起。那场比赛也相当精彩。 -- -- 《纽约》杂志特约编辑威尔·莱奇

paragraphs.speaker: Magazine
paragraphs.en: It's incredible to watch the streets everywhere. You know, I lived in New York for 13 years. My old street packed with people. And what was amazing about that comeback last night, the vibes had been so off since Monday. This -- the scenes of -- of all those people in the street had been replaced by scowling billionaires courtside. Right? Like -- like scowling celebrities and upset people.
paragraphs.cn: 看到每条街道上的人群，真是难以置信。我在纽约住了13年。我以前的街道挤满了人。昨晚那次逆转的惊人之处在于，自周一以来气氛一直很糟糕。街上的人群被场边皱眉的亿万富翁取代了，对吧？就像皱眉的名人和沮丧的人们。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: No one wants to see a scowling billionaire.
paragraphs.cn: 没人想看到皱眉的亿万富翁。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: And that all changed in the course of last night. There's a lot of them. There's a -- there's a lot -- a lot of leaders and a lot of celebrities out there. And they -- everyone looked very sad. I looked very sad. And so, to see -- to see that that comeback was really something incredible. And that O.G. Anunoby tip-in, it's something, frankly, that I place that piece that really not just Knicks fans will remember forever, but really NBA fans. That's an NBA historic play.
paragraphs.cn: 而这一切在昨晚发生了改变。有很多这样的人。有很多领袖和名人。他们每个人看起来都很悲伤。我也看起来很悲伤。所以，看到那次逆转真是不可思议。而OG·阿奴诺比的那次补篮，坦率地说，我认为那不仅会让尼克斯球迷永远记住，而且所有NBA球迷都会记住。那是一个NBA历史性的时刻。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Yes. Absolutely. And it was quite a game. I mean, I -- full disclosure, I had to watch the highlights this morning and read about it, because some of us had to go to bed early. But you know, the fact that this was this 29-point comeback. Just -- just set the scene for us. What are you seeing going into game five from both teams?
paragraphs.cn: 是的，绝对如此。那真是一场精彩的比赛。我的意思是，坦白说，我今早才看了集锦和报道，因为我们有些人得早睡。但你知道，这是一场29分的大逆转。给我们描述一下场景吧。进入第五场比赛，你从两队身上看到了什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: Certainly, you know, they'll go back to San Antonio for game five. And so, the next game won't have the Garden environment. But the Knicks just won two games in San Antonio. And you know, the Spurs are obviously incredibly talented. I think everyone assumes Victor Wembanyama is going to win several championships throughout his career. But they are very, very young. And I think that was kind of revealed last night.
paragraphs.cn: 当然，他们会回到圣安东尼奥打第五场。所以下一场比赛不会有花园球馆的氛围。但尼克斯刚刚在圣安东尼奥赢了两场。你知道，马刺显然天赋异禀。我想每个人都认为维克托·文班亚马会在职业生涯中赢得多个总冠军。但他们非常非常年轻。我认为昨晚这一点暴露了出来。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: Wembanyama even talked about that, about how they felt like they'd lost their hunger in the fourth quarter. So, I think that we'll see. That's a lot to overcome. Generally, historically speaking, when you have that kind of loss or just a heart -- rips the heart out of your chest loss --
paragraphs.cn: 文班亚马甚至谈到了这一点，说他们在第四节失去了饥饿感。所以，我想我们会看到的。那很难克服。一般来说，从历史角度看，当你遭遇那样的失利，或者那种撕心裂肺的失利 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: -- like the Spurs just had, it's very difficult to recover from, even -- even if you -- you're at home. But even if they lose that game, the Knicks will be back for game six. I can't imagine what the Garden would be like for a game six.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 就像马刺刚刚经历的那样，很难恢复过来，即使你在主场。但即使他们输了那场，尼克斯也会回来打第六场。我无法想象花园球馆在第六场会是什么样子。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Oh, wow. Yes. Me either. We'll deal with that when, if and when we come to it. Let's talk a little soccer, shall we? Football, depending on where you are, you know, in the rest of the world. This has been tough, heading into this moment: between ticket prices, immigration issues this week. First match today in Mexico City. Are we now getting into the heart of what this tournament is about?
paragraphs.cn: 哦，哇。是的。我也无法想象。到时候再说吧。我们来谈谈足球，好吗？取决于你在世界哪个地方，这届赛事从一开始就很艰难：票价问题、这周的移民问题。今天在墨西哥城的第一场比赛。我们现在是否进入了这项赛事的核心？

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: I mean, I think certainly FIFA hopes so, because this has really been a real mess for them. You know. this was -- after Russia eight years ago and Qatar four years ago, this was supposed to be the World Cup that went smoothly. They've been waiting for -- to get back to North America, you know, for -- since 1994. Everything was supposed to be, like, this -- U.S. was a burgeoning soccer market. Everyone was supposed to be working out in that direction.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，我认为国际足联当然希望如此，因为这对他们来说真是一团糟。你知道，在八年前的俄罗斯和四年前的卡塔尔之后，这届世界杯本应顺利进行。他们一直期待着回到北美，自从1994年以来。一切本应如此 -- -- 美国是一个蓬勃发展的足球市场。每个人都应该朝着那个方向努力。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: And it just -- it's been the opposite of that in every possible way. The hope is -- and frankly, historically, there is some signs that this happens -- once the ball is kicked --
paragraphs.cn: 而实际上，它完全相反。希望是 -- -- 坦率地说，从历史上看，有一些迹象表明这种情况会发生 -- -- 一旦球被踢出 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: -- and once things get going, and once this incredible experience -- you know, one of the great things about the World Cup is that we'll see. And remember, there's 16 more nations involved in this one. Every game takes on its own unique personality. The personality of the fan bases. And, you know, these games are still going to be -- the stands are still going to be full. As high as the ticket prices have been, these stands are generally.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 一旦事情开始进行，一旦这种不可思议的经历 -- -- 你知道，世界杯的伟大之处之一就是我们将会看到。记住，这次有16个更多的国家参与。每场比赛都有其独特的个性。球迷群体的个性。而且，你知道，这些比赛仍然会 -- -- 看台上仍然会坐满人。尽管票价很高，但看台通常都是满的。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: And Azteca today with the Mexico game, that's going to be an incredible environment. The opening of the World Cup at the Azteca with the Mexican team. These fans are -- like, every game takes on the personality of these varying fan base. The entire world is looking at this. Maybe the U.S. is not maybe looking at its best right now, hosting this tournament, in a lot of ways.
paragraphs.cn: 而今天阿兹特克体育场的墨西哥比赛，那将是一个不可思议的环境。世界杯在阿兹特克体育场开幕，墨西哥队出战。这些球迷 -- -- 就像每场比赛都带有不同球迷群体的个性。全世界都在关注。也许美国现在在很多方面并不是以最佳状态来主办这次赛事。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: But once the game gets going, certainly, the hope is among not just FIFA, but I think a lot of soccer fans, that a lot of this will fade away a little bit. But you know, we'll see.
paragraphs.cn: 但一旦比赛开始，当然，希望不仅在国际足联内部，而且我认为很多足球迷都希望，很多这些问题会逐渐消退。但你知道，我们拭目以待。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Yes, yes. As long as you don't have to, you know, pay for a train ticket into New Jersey.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，是的。只要你不必，你知道，付钱买一张去新泽西的火车票。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: When we -- you talked about the stands -- stands being full, rather. What are the chances that some of those -- there are still tickets available, right? That they start to drop to make sure that the stands are full?
paragraphs.cn: 当我们 -- -- 你谈到看台 -- -- 看台坐满。那么，有些比赛 -- -- 仍然有票可买，对吧？票价开始下降以确保看台坐满的可能性有多大？

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: I think they will. You know, I'm -- I'm based outside of Atlanta. There's a game between Uzbekistan and Qatar that takes place at 7:30 on a Wednesday. The get-in price is $400. Now I -- I suppose it's possible there's more Uzbekistan fans outside of Mercedes-Benz stadium in Atlanta than I think, but I suspect that's really because FIFA's kind of controlling the market a little bit on this.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为会下降。你知道，我 -- -- 我住在亚特兰大郊区。有一场乌兹别克斯坦对卡塔尔的比赛，在周三晚上7:30举行。入场票价是400美元。现在，我 -- -- 我想可能亚特兰大梅赛德斯-奔驰体育场外的乌兹别克斯坦球迷比我想象的要多，但我怀疑这实际上是因为国际足联在某种程度上控制了市场。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: So, controlling the resale market, as well. They're not going to want empty stands. I think that once these game gets going, once I think some of the realism that they do not want to see empty stands, I think you're going to see some of these prices go down. Now, I don't want to imply that, all of a sudden, you're going to be getting $5 tickets to these games.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，也控制了转售市场。他们不想看到空看台。我认为一旦比赛开始，一旦他们意识到不想看到空看台，你会看到一些票价下降。现在，我不想暗示你会突然得到5美元的比赛门票。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: But some of these obscene prices, given prices that you're seeing, I do think you'll fall. Now, not for the larger games, right? Like obviously, the finals still going to be. Spain.
paragraphs.cn: 但一些离谱的价格，考虑到你看到的价格，我确实认为会下降。当然，不是针对大型比赛，对吧？比如，决赛仍然会很贵。西班牙。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: You know, a lot -- a lot of the larger teams, Brazil, those are all going to be sold out and very expensive. But I think there's 48 teams in this thing. There's a lot of games. If you want to be able to just say, I went to a World Cup game, a lot of these prices are going to fall. I think for -- for a reasonable amount to at least get in and see what's going on.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，很多 -- -- 很多强队，比如巴西，那些比赛都会售罄并且非常昂贵。但我认为这次有48支球队。有很多比赛。如果你只是想说我去了世界杯比赛，很多票价会下降。我认为至少以合理的价格入场看看发生了什么。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: That's great. And you know, a watch party is always fun, too, having been to a couple.
paragraphs.cn: 那太好了。而且你知道，观看派对也总是很有趣，我去过几次。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: You know, almost 30 years ago when the World Cup was in France.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，差不多30年前，世界杯在法国举行的时候。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Really good time. Will, appreciate it. Thank you.
paragraphs.cn: 那段时光真的很愉快。威尔，感谢你。谢谢。

paragraphs.speaker: Leitch
paragraphs.en: Thanks for having me.
paragraphs.cn: 感谢你的邀请。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Straight ahead here on CNN THIS MORNING, President Trump's unique take on rising costs for Americans. Why he loves the latest inflation news. And a stabbing, sparking days of unrest now in Northern Ireland. Why officials are worried this violence could spread. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 接下来在CNN今晨节目中，特朗普总统对美国民众生活成本上涨的独特看法。他为何喜欢最新的通胀数据。还有一起刺伤事件，在北爱尔兰引发了持续数日的骚乱。官员们为何担心暴力可能蔓延。

paragraphs.speaker: Erica Hill
paragraphs.en: Good morning. Half past the hour here on CNN THIS MORNING. I'm Erica Hill. Nice to have you with us today. Here's a look at what's happening right now. Violent anti-immigration protests are taking over Belfast. The protests in northern Ireland sparked after a Sudanese man was charged in a stabbing attack. Police now using water cannons against crowds as masked rioters, were told as well, broke into some homes, setting fire to buildings and cars, targeting immigrants.
paragraphs.cn: 早上好。现在是CNN今晨节目，整点过半小时。我是埃里卡·希尔。很高兴今天有你的陪伴。以下是正在发生的事件。反移民暴力抗议活动正在占领贝尔法斯特。北爱尔兰的抗议活动是在一名苏丹男子被指控参与刺伤袭击后引发的。警方现在使用水炮对付人群，蒙面暴徒据称还闯入了一些住宅，纵火烧毁建筑物和汽车，目标针对移民。

paragraphs.speaker: Erica Hill
paragraphs.en: Northern Ireland's first minister calls the violence "outright thuggery." President Trump is directing Republican lawmakers to deliver a massive $350 billion cash infusion to the Pentagon, and also demanding they push through an election overhaul bill that has stalled now for weeks in the Senate. In a Truth Social post, Trump called on Republicans to act immediately with, quote, "no games, no delays and no weak promises." Another round of U.S. military strikes against targets across Iran now finished, but U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 北爱尔兰首席大臣称这种暴力行为是"彻头彻尾的暴行"。特朗普总统正指示共和党议员向五角大楼注入3500亿美元的巨额资金，并要求他们推动一项选举改革法案，该法案已在参议院搁置数周。在Truth Social的一篇帖子中，特朗普呼吁共和党人立即采取行动，并称"不要玩游戏，不要拖延，不要空头承诺"。美国对伊朗境内目标的又一轮军事打击现已结束，但美国

paragraphs.speaker: Erica Hill
paragraphs.en: Central Command says that round of Tomahawk cruise missiles was targeting military sites across Iran. Iran is vowing to retaliate with new attacks against U.S. military targets. Soaring global energy costs, the war with Iran, both putting increased pressure on the U.S. economy. And the newest Consumer Price Index report shows inflation hitting 4.2 percent in May. That is the highest rise for inflation in three years. As for President Trump, he loves it.
paragraphs.cn: 中央司令部表示，那轮战斧巡航导弹是针对伊朗境内的军事目标。伊朗誓言要以新的袭击报复美国军事目标。全球能源成本飙升以及与伊朗的战争，都给美国经济带来了更大压力。最新的消费者价格指数报告显示，5月份通胀率达到4.2%。这是三年来通胀涨幅最高的一次。至于特朗普总统，他对此表示喜欢。

paragraphs.en: Are you concerned, Mr. President, about the latest inflation number, which came out this morning. Could that be a negative for Republicans who are trying to hold the House and Senate?
paragraphs.cn: 总统先生，您是否担心今天上午公布的最新通胀数据？这对试图保住众议院和参议院席位的共和党人来说会不会是一个负面因素？

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.en: No, I love it. The numbers were great. You know what I really love? I love the inflation.
paragraphs.cn: 不，我喜欢它。这些数字很棒。你知道我真正喜欢什么吗？我喜欢通胀。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Joining me now in the group chat, Aaron Blake, CNN senior reporter, Mike Dubke, former White House -- Trump White House communications director, and Meghan Hays, former Biden White House director of message planning. It's lovely to see all of you this morning. I want to play some of the reaction that we heard to President Trump's comments when he was asked about inflation. Specifically, here's what we heard from Marjorie Taylor Greene on those comments. Take a listen.
paragraphs.cn: 现在加入我们群聊的有CNN高级记者亚伦·布莱克、前白宫（特朗普白宫）通讯主任迈克·杜布克，以及前拜登白宫信息规划主任梅根·海斯。今天早上很高兴见到各位。我想播放一些我们听到的对特朗普总统关于通胀问题的评论的反应。具体来说，这是马乔里·泰勒·格林对那些评论的看法。请听。

paragraphs.speaker: Marjorie Taylor Greene
paragraphs.en: It's pretty hard to take from a billionaire president when he says he loves inflation brought on the American people because of a war that he decided to pursue. And, you know, that one's going to come back and bite him. He basically just handed the Democrats a great big campaign ad for the 2026 midterms.
paragraphs.cn: 一位亿万富翁总统说他喜欢通胀，而通胀是因为他决定发动的战争给美国人民带来的，这很难让人接受。而且，你知道，这句话会反噬他的。他基本上就是给民主党送上了一个2026年中期选举的绝佳竞选广告。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Mike, is it, in fact, the great big campaign ad for Democrats?
paragraphs.cn: 迈克，这真的是给民主党的大竞选广告吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Mike Dubke
paragraphs.en: Oh, it's absolutely going to be a big, fat campaign ad for Democrats. They are going to use it. I think, with the president in context, he was basically quoting blood, sweat and tears. What goes up must come down. I think that's -- he's assuming that once we cease hostilities with Iran and oil prices come down, that the momentum going into the midterm elections is going to create a situation where people feel good about the economy.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，这绝对是给民主党的一个大而有力的竞选广告。他们会利用这一点。我认为，结合总统的语境，他基本上是在引用"血、汗、泪"。有涨必有跌。我认为 -- -- 他假设一旦我们停止与伊朗的敌对行动，油价下跌，那么进入中期选举的势头将创造一种人们对经济感觉良好的局面。

paragraphs.speaker: Mike Dubke
paragraphs.en: The unfortunate part, I think, right now is that runway, until we get to the midterms, is shrinking. And so, we really -- we -- if that's going to happen, it's got to happen soon.
paragraphs.cn: 不幸的是，我认为现在距离中期选举的时间窗口正在缩小。所以，如果真的会发生，那必须尽快发生。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: So, as we wait to see for that, you know, whether and when that will happen, it's important to note, and, Aaron, you have a great piece out about this, this is not the first time the president says something that that doesn't land well, right? And perhaps most recently, I'm thinking of comments that he made where, you know, that he doesn't think about Americans when he -- and the economy, which he doubled down on when he was asked to clarify.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，在我们等待看是否以及何时会发生的同时，重要的是要注意到，亚伦，你有一篇关于这个问题的精彩文章，这不是总统第一次说一些效果不好的话，对吧？也许最近，我想到了他的一些言论，比如他在考虑经济时并不考虑美国人，而当被要求澄清时，他更是加倍坚持。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: The fact that this is not a one off for the president, Aaron, just walk us through where we're at.
paragraphs.cn: 这对总统来说不是一次性的，亚伦，请给我们讲讲我们现在的情况。

paragraphs.speaker: Aaron Blake
paragraphs.en: Yes, this has been a trend with President Trump I would say dating back to last year. We might remember at the time inflation was starting to become a real political problem for him. And he was supposed to go out and give some speeches and rallies in places like Pennsylvania, where he talked about affordability, and what he was going to do about these very real concerns that the American people had. And it just became clear that he didn't really want to talk about this.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我认为这是特朗普总统的一个趋势，可以追溯到去年。我们可能还记得，当时通胀开始成为他真正的政治问题。他本应出去在宾夕法尼亚等地发表演讲和集会，谈论负担能力以及他将如何应对美国人民非常现实的担忧。但很明显，他并不真的想谈论这个。

paragraphs.speaker: Aaron Blake
paragraphs.en: He was talking about affordability was a hoax and things like that. So, this has been a very long running problem for the White House and for Republicans' hopes in the midterms, where the president just treats these issues very flippantly. And the American people, if you look at the polling, have seen that and said that this guy is not taking this issue seriously enough. There are -- a CNN poll showed two-thirds of Americans think that the president is not taking inflation seriously.
paragraphs.cn: 他说负担能力是个骗局之类的话。所以，这对白宫和共和党在中期选举中的希望来说是一个长期存在的问题，总统对这些问题的处理非常轻率。而美国人民，如果你看民调，已经看到了这一点，并认为这家伙没有足够认真地对待这个问题。CNN的一项民调显示，三分之二的美国人认为总统没有认真对待通胀。

paragraphs.speaker: Aaron Blake
paragraphs.en: It was as high as three quarters in a CBS News poll that I've been watching for a while. So, this is a very real issue. And he does not seem to be changing his tack on this. And I think it's giving Republicans real heartburn right now.
paragraphs.cn: 在我关注了一段时间的CBS新闻民调中，这一比例高达四分之三。所以，这是一个非常现实的问题。他似乎并没有改变策略。我认为这正让共和党人非常头疼。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Meghan, all of that is happening. And we're seeing Republicans, right, who are acknowledging the pain that their constituents are feeling. We talked about this being an attack ad. The interesting note is, are you saying that Democrats are effectively harnessing that messaging?
paragraphs.cn: 梅根，这一切都在发生。我们看到共和党人正在承认他们的选民所感受到的痛苦。我们谈到这是一则攻击性广告。有趣的是，你是在说民主党人有效地利用了这种信息吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Meghan Hays
paragraphs.en: Yes, I think that they are. I also think the American people are just tired of Donald Trump and his flippant, callous remarks that he constantly makes. But they're more tired of Congress, who doesn't act. And they're not acting here. And so that -- they're the ones who are going to be up for re-election. Donald Trump's never going to be on the ballot again. So, he can say whatever he wants.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我认为他们确实如此。我还认为美国人民已经厌倦了唐纳德·特朗普和他不断发表的轻率、冷酷的言论。但他们更厌倦了不采取行动的国会。而国会现在没有行动。所以，他们才是要面临连任的人。唐纳德·特朗普再也不会出现在选票上了。所以，他想说什么就说什么。

paragraphs.speaker: Meghan Hays
paragraphs.en: But the fact that Congress isn't acting and these Republicans aren't being a check to Donald Trump, they are the ones who are going to lose. So, I think the more he says and the crazier statements he makes and Republicans stand by and just let it happen are the ones that are going to lose these re-elections. And that's when you're going to see a bigger sweep come in the midterms.
paragraphs.cn: 但事实是，国会没有行动，这些共和党人也没有制衡唐纳德·特朗普，他们才是会输的人。所以，我认为他说得越多，发表的言论越疯狂，而共和党人袖手旁观、听之任之，这些人就会在连任中失败。那时你就会看到中期选举中更大的横扫。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Let's take a look at another story we're following very closely. President Trump refusing to back down from his trip -- his pick, rather, for interim director of national intelligence. You've got Democrats, of course, threatening to allow FISA to lapse if Bill Pulte is appointed. This is a critical surveillance tool, as we know, used by intelligence officials for national security. The president's backers trying to make the case here that Pulte is only interim. REP.
paragraphs.cn: 让我们看看我们正在密切关注的另一个故事。特朗普总统拒绝放弃他的行程 -- -- 更确切地说，是他对代理国家情报总监的人选。当然，民主党人威胁说，如果比尔·普尔特被任命，他们将允许《外国情报监视法》失效。我们知道，这是情报官员用于国家安全的关键监控工具。总统的支持者试图辩称普尔特只是临时人选。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: MIKE JOHNSON (R-LA): He is in the process of interviewing the persons who would potentially be the next DNI. They're going to be highly qualified. All these potential nominees are. We know that FISA would expire on Friday. We cannot allow that to happen. This should not be a partisan matter.
paragraphs.cn: 迈克·约翰逊（路易斯安那州共和党人）：他正在面试可能成为下一任国家情报总监的人选。他们将非常合格。所有这些潜在提名人都很合格。我们知道《外国情报监视法》将在周五到期。我们不能允许这种情况发生。这不应该是一个党派问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: On Tuesday, the president announced that Pulte would begin his role as acting DNI on June 19th. So, as we take a look at this one, there is bipartisan concern here, it's important to note, when it comes to Bill Pulte and what we are hearing. Mike, I'll kick this one off to you again to start.
paragraphs.cn: 周二，总统宣布普尔特将于6月19日开始担任代理国家情报总监。所以，当我们审视这个问题时，值得注意的是，两党对比尔·普尔特都存在担忧，正如我们所听到的。迈克，我将再次由你来开始讨论。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: How do you see this playing out in this moment?
paragraphs.cn: 你认为这件事目前会如何发展？

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: There's a lot at stake.
paragraphs.cn: 有很多利害关系。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.en: Here's the problem, though, with the setup on this. There is bipartisan concern about Pulte. But there's also bipartisan concern about FISA expiring. So, if there's this level of bipartisan concern, hopefully we would see some bipartisan action on this. Unfortunately, we're now in a game of chicken between the White House and Pulte and Democrats in Congress. So, my guess is, how does this play out? We're going to go through the weekend. We're going to have FISA expire on Friday.
paragraphs.cn: 但问题在于目前的局面。两党都对普尔特感到担忧。但两党也对《外国情报监视法》到期感到担忧。所以，如果存在这种程度的两党担忧，希望我们会看到一些两党行动。不幸的是，我们现在陷入了白宫、普尔特和国会民主党人之间的懦夫博弈。所以，我的猜测是，这会如何发展？我们将度过这个周末。《外国情报监视法》将在周五到期。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.en: Before Congress leaves for the -- either the June 19th break or the Fourth of July break, we're going to see some movement on this. The president's going to appoint somebody. Something else is going to happen. But we're now in this classic game of Washington chicken, and nobody's winning.
paragraphs.cn: 在国会休会之前 -- -- 无论是6月19日的休会还是7月4日的休会 -- -- 我们会看到一些进展。总统会任命某人。还会发生其他事情。但我们现在陷入了经典的华盛顿懦夫博弈，没有人获胜。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: In terms of the Washington chicken, Meghan, how much do you think could actually be happening behind the scenes between Democrats and Republicans, given that there is bipartisan concern and, in some cases, outrage?
paragraphs.cn: 关于华盛顿的鸡，梅根，考虑到两党都有担忧，甚至在某些情况下是愤怒，你认为民主党和共和党之间在幕后可能有多少实际动作？

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.en: Yes, I think that there's a lot happening behind the scenes. People -- members that I've talked to on The Hill have, you know, they're -- everyone is working closely to get either a new nominee or to get this passed. Both things can be true here, right? FISA shouldn't expire and Pulte shouldn't be in DNI at all. I mean, I think Democrats have real concern about election integrity and security here and the way he has weaponized his role already. And then using the intelligence that he'll have access to.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我认为幕后有很多动作。我在国会山交谈过的议员们，你知道，他们 -- -- 每个人都在密切合作，要么是为了获得新的提名人，要么是为了让这个通过。这两件事可能同时成立，对吧？FISA不应该过期，而普尔特根本不应该担任国家情报总监。我的意思是，我认为民主党人对选举的完整性和安全性有真正的担忧，以及他已经在利用他的角色。然后利用他将能接触到的情报。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.en: So, there are some real concerns here, but there are real concerns of letting FISA expire. So, I would imagine that there is significant negotiations going on behind the scenes. And I agree with Mike, that they'll have some sort of solution, whether it expires, and then they name someone very quickly. But this will -- this will resolve itself in Washington fashion, and it'll be very dramatic.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，这里有一些真正的担忧，但让FISA过期也有真正的担忧。所以，我想象幕后正在进行重要的谈判。我同意迈克的说法，他们会找到某种解决方案，无论是让它过期，然后他们很快任命某人。但这会 -- -- 这会以华盛顿的方式解决，而且会非常戏剧化。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Because it will have -- Washington fashion is dramatic? Did I hear that correctly?
paragraphs.cn: 因为它会有 -- -- 华盛顿的方式是戏剧化的？我没听错吧？

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: A little bit of drama. Aaron, I'll give you one last bit to weigh in on that. As we watch all this, I mean, just, if you could, put it in perspective. You know, people hear this tossed around, right, there are the concerns about the DNI and the fact that he doesn't have the extensive national security experience that is required of that job. And they hear FISA. But the reality for most Americans, why does this matter to them?
paragraphs.cn: 有点戏剧性。亚伦，我给你最后一点时间来谈谈这个。当我们观察这一切时，我的意思是，如果你能，把它放在背景中。你知道，人们听到这些被抛来抛去，对吧，有关于国家情报总监的担忧，以及他缺乏该职位所需的广泛国家安全经验。他们听到FISA。但对大多数美国人来说，现实是，这为什么对他们重要？

paragraphs.speaker: Blake
paragraphs.en: I mean, this is a very significant spying authority that the United States government uses overseas to prevent, you know, threats that could attack the homeland. So, I think that creates a situation in which, you know, Democrats want to hold the line on this. They want to -- they want to be very firm when it comes to opposing Bill Pulte for the DNI job.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，这是美国政府用于海外防止可能攻击本土的威胁的非常重要的监视权力。所以，我认为这造成了一种局面，你知道，民主党人想在这件事上坚守立场。他们想 -- -- 他们想在反对比尔·普尔特担任国家情报总监一事上非常坚定。

paragraphs.speaker: Blake
paragraphs.en: But, when you do that, if you're the ones that are going to wind up holding off these spying authorities and letting them lapse, even for a small period of time, that is a risk if something were to happen. This is a very significant thing for the United States government. That's why there's such a big debate about this right now.
paragraphs.cn: 但是，当你这样做时，如果你是最终导致这些监视权力被搁置甚至短暂失效的人，那么如果发生什么事，这就是一个风险。这对美国政府来说是一件非常重要的事情。这就是为什么现在有这么大的争论。

paragraphs.speaker: Blake
paragraphs.en: And so, it's going to be interesting to see whether those Democrats are actually going to be able to all hold the line, or if we might see some break ranks and say, OK, well, let's allow a short-term extension just while we negotiate this and figure out this whole Bill Pulte situation. I do think it's notable that you're seeing in those comments from Mike Johnson and from the White House, they're telegraphing that this Pulte thing is a very short term thing, that they're looking for a permanent director.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，看看那些民主党人是否真的能全部坚守立场，或者我们是否会看到一些人打破阵线说，好吧，让我们允许一个短期延期，同时我们谈判并解决整个比尔·普尔特的情况，这将很有趣。我确实认为值得注意的是，你在迈克·约翰逊和白宫的评论中看到，他们在暗示普尔特这件事是非常短期的，他们正在寻找一个永久性的局长。

paragraphs.speaker: Blake
paragraphs.en: Whether that's good enough for Democrats to move forward and support this short term extension, I think that's a really big question right now.
paragraphs.cn: 这对民主党人来说是否足够好，让他们继续前进并支持这个短期延期，我认为这是目前一个非常大的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Yes. It is interesting to see those comments as they try to downplay, in many ways, by saying, not a lot to see here, it's just an interim move. That certainly hasn't been enough for some, as we know. One other thing we wanted to get your take on this morning. There has been a lot made about how the president is doing during certain meetings and moments, including this. REP. SYDNEY KAMLAGER-DOVE (D-CA): I rise today to sound the alarm about a grave national security threat, Donald Trump's sleeping habits.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。看到这些评论很有趣，因为他们试图在很多方面淡化，说没什么可看的，这只是个临时举措。我们知道，这对一些人来说当然还不够。今天早上我们还想听听你的看法。有很多关于总统在某些会议和时刻的表现的讨论，包括这个。众议员悉尼·卡姆拉格-多夫（加州民主党）：我今天站起来对一项严重的国家安全威胁发出警报，那就是唐纳德·特朗普的睡眠习惯。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: I know we have rules about decorum, so I will stick to the facts, because this is, in fact, a serious matter of oversight, transparency and national security. We cannot have a president asleep on the job. Is he sleeping in classified briefings, on calls with foreign leaders? How can we trust a president who is literally asleep at the wheel to make informed decisions about national security?
paragraphs.cn: 我知道我们有关于礼仪的规定，所以我会坚持事实，因为这实际上是一个涉及监督、透明度和国家安全的严肃问题。我们不能有一个在任上睡觉的总统。他是在机密简报会上睡觉，还是在与外国领导人通话时睡觉？我们怎么能信任一个在方向盘上睡着的总统来做出关于国家安全的明智决定？

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Meghan, you get first crack at this one. Is it a national security threat?
paragraphs.cn: 梅根，你先来回答这个问题。这是国家安全威胁吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.en: I mean, it's a national security threat that he's not paying attention, yes, but there is a massive apparatus around the president when these decisions are made. Whether or not you trust the people that are around the president is a different story. I just think this is rich coming from a White House that criticized Joe Biden so much in calling him "Sleepy Joe Biden." So, I mean, if I was Donald Trump, I would be embarrassed. Also, you're president of the United States.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，他不专心确实是一个国家安全威胁，但在做出这些决定时，总统周围有一个庞大的机构。你是否信任总统身边的人则是另一回事。我只是觉得，来自一个曾大肆批评乔·拜登并称其为"瞌睡乔·拜登"的白宫，这很讽刺。所以，如果我是唐纳德·特朗普，我会感到尴尬。而且，你是美国总统。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.en: I don't understand why you can't stay awake during daylight hours to do your job.
paragraphs.cn: 我不明白你为什么不能在白天保持清醒来工作。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Marco Rubio has said he's awake, not just during the daytime, but also all throughout the night. That he hears from him regularly in the wee hours. We do see the posts in the wee hours. Mike, but they're, you know, legitimate questions, right? There's a lot of criticism about what wasn't said about Joe Biden. Is this a fair game?
paragraphs.cn: 马可·卢比奥说他不仅白天醒着，而且整晚都醒着。他说他经常在凌晨收到总统的消息。我们确实看到凌晨的帖子。迈克，但这些都是合理的问题，对吧？关于乔·拜登，有很多批评没有被提及。这是公平的游戏吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.en: This -- this is -- sure, everything's fair game, but this is political theater. And Marco Rubio is absolutely correct. I got calls -- when I was in the White House, I got calls at 2:00 in the morning from the president. The man does not sleep. He does keep odd hours. And I'm not -- I am really not concerned with him closing his eyes and Democrats jumping on a couple of, you know, photographs of his -- of his eyes closed in this setting. This is -- this is theater in this -- in this particular case.
paragraphs.cn: 这 -- -- 这是 -- -- 当然，一切都是公平的，但这是政治作秀。马可·卢比奥说得完全正确。我在白宫时，凌晨两点接到过总统的电话。这个人不睡觉。他的作息时间确实不规律。我并不担心他闭上眼睛，也不担心民主党抓住几张他在这种场合闭眼的照片。这 -- -- 这在这个特定情况下是作秀。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: My grandfather used to say he was just checking his eyelids for holes. I'm just saying. Maybe, you know --
paragraphs.cn: 我祖父常说，他只是在检查眼皮上有没有洞。我只是说说而已。也许，你知道 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: There you go. Still to come here on CNN THIS MORNING, Republicans just passed the immigration funding bill after a nearly 80-day partial government shutdown. What does this do? Did Democrats lose their leverage? Plus.
paragraphs.cn: 就是这样。CNN《今晨》节目接下来，共和党人在政府部分停摆近80天后刚刚通过了移民拨款法案。这意味着什么？民主党是否失去了筹码？另外。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: You had some of Trump's most senior advisors, the top level of the government, gathered in the Situation Room.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普的一些最资深顾问，政府最高层，聚集在战情室。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: The latest excerpts from a forthcoming book revealing how President Trump's closest advisers reportedly tried to manage the Jeffrey Epstein fallout and the release of the president's name.
paragraphs.cn: 一本即将出版的书籍的最新摘录揭示了特朗普总统最亲密的顾问据称如何试图处理杰弗里·爱泼斯坦的后果以及总统名字的曝光。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: A nearly $70 billion boost to President Trump's immigration and deportation agenda. Trump signing that bill into law yesterday, which ends now a six-month fight over funding for the Department of Homeland Security, which began after the shooting deaths of two U.S. citizens, Alex Pretti and Renee Good. Now that bill provides $38 billion for ICE, $26 billion for Border Patrol and an additional $5 billion for unseen costs. The funding is set to last for the next three years.
paragraphs.cn: 特朗普总统的移民与驱逐议程获得了近700亿美元的资金支持。特朗普昨日签署了该法案，结束了为期六个月关于国土安全部资金的争斗。这场争斗始于两名美国公民亚历克斯·普雷蒂和蕾妮·古德被枪杀之后。该法案为移民和海关执法局（ICE）提供380亿美元，为边境巡逻队提供260亿美元，另外还有50亿美元用于未预见成本。这笔资金将持续三年。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Keep in mind here, typically it would only be funded for one year at a time. It was also passed without the support of a single Democrat. REP. MIKE JOHNSON (R-LA): The American people are going to remember it. They're going to remember, too, that it was Republicans and Republicans alone who did the responsible thing and funded these critically important agencies at this critical time.
paragraphs.cn: 需要注意的是，通常这类资金每年只拨付一次。该法案也没有得到任何民主党人的支持。路易斯安那州共和党众议员迈克·约翰逊表示："美国人民会记住这一点。他们也会记住，是共和党人，而且只有共和党人，在这个关键时刻做了负责任的事，为这些至关重要的机构提供了资金。"

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Democrats, on the other hand, demonstrated to the entire country once again how little they prioritize the security of our borders and the livelihoods of those who put their own lives at risk and on the line to defend our homeland.
paragraphs.cn: "另一方面，民主党人再次向全国展示了他们对边境安全以及那些冒着生命危险保卫国土的人的生计是多么不重视。"

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: I want to bring in now Democratic Congressman James Walkinshaw of Virginia, who joins us this morning. Nice to have you with us. When we look at where things stand, in terms of this funding, the fact that it was passed for three years, this essentially shields the agencies from the appropriations process. So, what oversight will Congress have over the course of these next three years? REP.
paragraphs.cn: 现在让我们连线弗吉尼亚州民主党众议员詹姆斯·沃金肖，他今早加入我们。欢迎您。当我们审视这笔资金的现状时，它被批准了三年，这实际上使这些机构免受拨款程序的影响。那么，在未来三年内，国会将如何进行监督？

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: JAMES WALKINSHAW (D-VA): Well, look, I'm certainly hopeful that there'll be a change in control of Congress in January, which would allow us to conduct robust oversight in the form of hearings, subpoenas when necessary, depositions when necessary. But currently, this Republican congress is quite consistent that they're not going to provide real oversight of the reckless, out-of-control mass deportation agenda that we have seen.
paragraphs.cn: 詹姆斯·沃金肖（弗吉尼亚州民主党众议员）表示："嗯，我当然希望明年一月国会控制权会发生变化，这样我们就能通过听证会、必要时发出传票和取证等方式进行强有力的监督。但目前，这个共和党控制的国会非常一致，他们不会对我们所看到的鲁莽、失控的大规模驱逐议程进行真正的监督。"

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: So, I don't know that there will be much oversight for the remainder of this year, but I'm hopeful that in January 2027 we can get some oversight and bring this under control.
paragraphs.cn: "所以，我不知道今年剩余时间是否会有多少监督，但我希望2027年1月我们能进行一些监督，并控制住局面。"

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Among the list of demands that Democrats had, we have, of course, limits on face coverings, body camera requirements, clear use of force standards, which led to the record shutdown. Really, none of these demands made it into the final bill. Was it worth it?
paragraphs.cn: 在民主党提出的要求清单中，当然包括限制面罩、佩戴执法记录仪、明确使用武力标准等，这些导致了创纪录的政府停摆。实际上，这些要求没有一项被纳入最终法案。这值得吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Walkinshaw
paragraphs.en: Well, look, what Democrats said is federal law enforcement, ICE, CBP, should operate similarly to our local and state law enforcement, right? Have the same expectations of professionalism that we have of our local police. Republicans, led primarily by one man, Stephen Miller in the White House, said, no way, no how. So, from my perspective, it's always worth it to fight to protect the safety of the American people who were being terrorized by their own government, by masked agents surging into communities.
paragraphs.cn: "嗯，民主党人认为，联邦执法机构，如ICE和CBP，应该像地方和州执法机构一样运作，对吧？要有与地方警察相同的专业标准。而共和党人，主要由白宫的斯蒂芬·米勒领导，坚决反对。所以，在我看来，为保护美国人民的安全而斗争总是值得的，他们正被自己的政府、戴着面罩涌入社区的执法人员所恐吓。"

paragraphs.speaker: Walkinshaw
paragraphs.en: I think that fight is always worth it. We're going to continue that fight. We're going to have to take it to the ballot box in November. I think the mood and the polling across the country is quite clear that the American people, even those who might have supported the idea of stronger immigration enforcement that Donald Trump ran on, are looking at what they're seeing and saying, this ain't what I voted for. I didn't want to see kids with cancer deported.
paragraphs.cn: "我认为这场斗争总是值得的。我们将继续斗争。我们必须在11月的投票箱中解决这个问题。我认为全国的情绪和民调非常清楚，美国人民，甚至那些可能支持特朗普加强移民执法理念的人，看到现状后都在说，这不是我投票支持的。我不想看到患癌儿童被驱逐出境。"

paragraphs.speaker: Walkinshaw
paragraphs.en: I don't want to see pregnant women dragged through the street. I don't want to see American citizens shot dead and killed. The American people know this is out of control. Democrats are going to keep fighting to bring it under control.
paragraphs.cn: "我不想看到孕妇被拖过街道。我不想看到美国公民被枪杀。美国人民知道这已经失控了。民主党将继续努力控制局面。"

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Let's talk Epstein if we could. Bill Gates, of course, on Capitol Hill. You sat in that hearing yesterday, listened to his testimony. Representative Burchett, afterwards, came out and told the cameras that he had, in his view, very good coaching. That he had paid for very good coaching is what he said about Bill Gates. Did you find Bill Gates credible?
paragraphs.cn: 我们来谈谈爱泼斯坦吧。比尔·盖茨当然在国会山。你昨天参加了那场听证会，听取了他的证词。伯切特众议员后来出来对镜头说，他认为盖茨接受了非常好的指导。他说盖茨为非常好的指导付了钱。你觉得比尔·盖茨可信吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Walkinshaw
paragraphs.en: I did. I did find Bill Gates credible. Everything he said was consistent with what's in the files or in the public record. Look, it is an egregious error in judgment. It's the most charitable way to describe Bill Gates deciding to engage, meet with Jeffrey Epstein even after he knew of Jeffrey Epstein's 2008 guilty plea/conviction for a sex crime. Gates acknowledged that yesterday. He said that was a terrible, terrible mistake, and it certainly was.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我觉得比尔·盖茨可信。他说的每件事都与文件或公开记录一致。听着，这是一个严重的判断错误。这是对比尔·盖茨决定在已知杰弗里·爱泼斯坦2008年因性犯罪认罪/定罪后仍与其会面、交往的最宽容的描述。盖茨昨天承认了这一点。他说那是一个非常非常严重的错误，确实如此。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: When we look at where things stand, Congressman Burchett also calling for transparency, as you know. James Comer, the House Oversight chair, there has been some back and forth on what should be public. But he also talked about who he wanted to call in next. I just want to play that. REP. JAMES COMER (R-KY): I would like for Todd Blanche to come in, in July. Come in and let me -- let me be very clear. Todd Blanche came in a month ago.
paragraphs.cn: 当我们审视现状时，伯切特众议员也在呼吁透明度，如你所知。众议院监督委员会主席詹姆斯·科默在哪些内容应该公开的问题上有些反复。但他也谈到了接下来想传唤谁。我想播放一下。共和党众议员詹姆斯·科默：我希望托德·布兰奇在七月来。让他来 -- -- 让我说清楚。托德·布兰奇一个月前来过。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: He came in with Pam Bondi to answer questions from the Oversight Committee, and the Democrats stormed out. What a missed opportunity for the Democrats. But Todd Blanche answered questions that Republicans had for several hours.
paragraphs.cn: 他和帕姆·邦迪一起来回答监督委员会的问题，然后民主党人愤然离场。对民主党人来说，这是多么错失的机会。但托德·布兰奇回答了共和党人几个小时的提问。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: I'm curious, both your response to that and also what you feel you would get from Todd Blanche.
paragraphs.cn: 我很好奇，你对此有何回应，以及你认为能从托德·布兰奇那里得到什么。

paragraphs.speaker: Walkinshaw
paragraphs.en: In an effort to help Pam Bondi avoid having to come in and speak under oath and answer questions under oath, Chairman Comer tried to set up a secret, closed-door briefing with Bondi and Todd Blanche. That briefing would have had no or has no transcript. The media, the public would never know. In fact, he just described it. Do you have any idea what they discussed in that meeting? What questions were asked? You don't. It's part of the cover up.
paragraphs.cn: 为了帮助帕姆·邦迪避免在宣誓下作证并回答问题，科默主席试图安排与邦迪和布兰奇进行秘密的闭门简报会。那次简报会没有记录。媒体和公众永远不会知道。事实上，他刚刚描述了这一点。你知道他们在那个会议上讨论了什么吗？问了什么问题？你不知道。这是掩盖的一部分。

paragraphs.speaker: Walkinshaw
paragraphs.en: And Democrats said, look, we're not going to participate in secret behind door -- behind closed doors briefings as part of this coverup. We want Pam Bondi, under oath, in front of camera so the American people can judge it. And we want Todd Blanche, under oath, in front of cameras and let the American people, not the Oversight Committee members, decide whether they think he's telling the truth or not, about this massive cover up that the Trump administration has been engaged in around the Epstein files.
paragraphs.cn: 民主党人说，听着，我们不会参与这种作为掩盖一部分的秘密闭门简报会。我们希望帕姆·邦迪在宣誓下、在镜头前作证，让美国人民来评判。我们希望托德·布兰奇在宣誓下、在镜头前作证，让美国人民 -- -- 而不是监督委员会成员 -- -- 来决定他们是否认为他在特朗普政府围绕爱泼斯坦文件进行的大规模掩盖中说了真话。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: We will see if that happens. Certainly a lot of interest and a lot of questions. Congressman Walkinshaw, I appreciate you taking the time this morning. Thank you.
paragraphs.cn: 我们拭目以待。当然有很多兴趣和很多问题。沃金肖众议员，感谢您今早抽出时间。谢谢。

paragraphs.speaker: Walkinshaw
paragraphs.en: Thanks for having me.
paragraphs.cn: 谢谢邀请。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: While some of Jeffrey Epstein's associates are facing questions on Capitol Hill, we're also learning more about how the White House was attempting to deal with the fallout of this crisis last summer. New reporting from "New York Times" reporter Jonathan Swan and Maggie Haberman details how Trump's top advisers assembled high-level meetings to talk about the growing crisis without the president.
paragraphs.cn: 在杰弗里·爱泼斯坦的一些同伙在国会山面临质询的同时，我们也了解到更多关于白宫去年夏天试图处理这场危机后果的信息。《纽约时报》记者乔纳森·斯旺和玛吉·哈伯曼的新报道详细描述了特朗普的高级顾问如何召集高层会议，讨论在没有总统参与的情况下日益严重的危机。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: JONATHAN SWAN, WHITE HOUSE REPORTER, "NEW YORK TIMES": So, over the summer of 2025, you had some of Trump's most senior advisors, at the top level of the government, gathered in the Situation Room.
paragraphs.cn: 《纽约时报》白宫记者乔纳森·斯旺：所以，在2025年夏天，特朗普的一些最高级别顾问，政府高层，聚集在战情室。

paragraphs.speaker: Maggie Haberman
paragraphs.en: Which is typically used for national security or responding to national disasters.
paragraphs.cn: 战情室通常用于处理国家安全或应对国家灾难。

paragraphs.speaker: Swan
paragraphs.en: To figure out a way to spin their way out of this.
paragraphs.cn: 为了想办法摆脱困境。

paragraphs.speaker: Haberman
paragraphs.en: Not with the president, but among themselves. The discussion in the Situation Room was not about how to give these women a hearing or a chance to tell their stories. These meetings were about how to handle the political fallout around the White House.
paragraphs.cn: 不是和总统一起，而是他们自己之间。战情室的讨论不是关于如何让这些女性有机会陈述她们的故事。这些会议是关于如何处理白宫周围的政治后果。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: The group chat is back. Look, it's a fascinating read, an excerpt from their forthcoming book, as we look at the way all of this is playing out. Aaron, one of the many things that stood out to me, it was not only the players that were involved, but how they were responding to these moments in public versus in private. I think Dan Bongino may be the best example. In public, pretty measured. In private, the exact opposite. And had some pretty stark warnings for officials.
paragraphs.cn: 群聊又回来了。看，这是一篇引人入胜的读物，是他们即将出版的新书节选，我们看看这一切是如何展开的。亚伦，让我印象深刻的事情之一，不仅是涉及的参与者，还有他们在公开场合与私下里对这些时刻的反应。我认为丹·邦吉诺可能是最好的例子。在公开场合，相当克制。私下里，完全相反。而且对官员们提出了一些相当严厉的警告。

paragraphs.speaker: Blake
paragraphs.en: Yes, it was pretty remarkable to see some of the quotes from Dan Bongino talking about his superior, who is Attorney General Pam Bondi, and her handling of this. Basically her botched handling of this. Of course, she was out there on TV in the months before talking about, you know, she was asked about the client list. She said, it's on my desk. She handed out those binders that turned out to be rather old information about this. There was very much concern about the politics of this internally.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，看到丹·邦吉诺的一些言论非常引人注目，他谈论他的上司，司法部长帕姆·邦迪，以及她对此事的处理。基本上是她的拙劣处理。当然，她在之前的几个月里在电视上被问到客户名单时，她说，名单就在我桌上。她分发了那些后来被证明是相当陈旧信息的活页夹。内部对此的政治问题非常担忧。

paragraphs.speaker: Blake
paragraphs.en: And clearly these politics were taken very seriously. One of the things I was really struck by was, these meetings included high level officials at the Justice Department. You remember -- might remember back in the Obama administration there was a big controversy about Attorney General Loretta Lynch meeting with Bill Clinton on the tarmac of an airport during the Hillary Clinton email investigation.
paragraphs.cn: 显然，这些政治问题被非常认真地对待。让我非常震惊的一件事是，这些会议包括司法部的高级官员。你可能还记得奥巴马政府时期，司法部长洛蕾塔·林奇在希拉里·克林顿电子邮件调查期间在机场停机坪与比尔·克林顿会面引发巨大争议。

paragraphs.speaker: Blake
paragraphs.en: Well, now we have this reporting that basically the Justice Department is strategizing with the White House about how to insulate the president from this budding kind of scandal last year. And it kind of seems par for course for what's been happening in recent years.
paragraphs.cn: 现在我们有报道称，司法部基本上是在与白宫制定策略，以保护总统免受去年这种初现端倪的丑闻影响。这似乎与近年来发生的事情如出一辙。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: The other thing that struck me in this moment were the involvement of Todd Blanche, Meghan, especially as we were just talking about, right, what could potentially be learned from Todd Blanche if, in fact, there were more questions from the House Oversight Committee.
paragraphs.cn: 另一件让我印象深刻的事情是托德·布兰奇的参与，梅根，尤其是我们刚才谈到的，对吧，如果众议院监督委员会真的提出更多问题，那么从托德·布兰奇那里可能了解到什么。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Given his involvement and his suggestions in those meetings and where he is now and the fact that Pam Bondi, right, was saying, just in the last several days, well, Todd Blanche was responsible for everything related to the release here, it's fascinating the way this all sets it up.
paragraphs.cn: 考虑到他在那些会议中的参与和他的建议，以及他现在的位置，还有帕姆·邦迪就在前几天说，托德·布兰奇负责与此次发布相关的一切，这一切的设定真是引人入胜。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.en: Yes, absolutely. I mean, I think that Blanche is going to have a hard time getting out of committee anyways, and he's going to have to answer some questions about the Epstein files if he wants to get confirmed, regardless of this story that just came out. But I also think, I mean, they said that they talked to over 1,000 people I know for the whole book. But this is a lot of people who are no longer with the administration trying to make themselves look better when they were all at fault.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，绝对如此。我的意思是，我认为布兰奇无论如何都很难从委员会中脱身，而且无论这篇报道如何，他如果想获得确认，就必须回答一些关于爱泼斯坦文件的问题。但我也认为，他们说过为了整本书采访了超过1000人。但这些人中有很多已不在政府任职，他们试图在都有过错的情况下让自己看起来更好。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.en: This whole handling of this has been disgusting and gross. And I, you know, I've been in the Situation Room for meetings. So has Mike. These are -- that is a sacred place where you have national security conversations. So, the fact that they are talking about how to get Donald Trump out of being in the Epstein files is just gross to me and a complete misuse of those facilities in the White House. But the -- this is a lot of people just trying to make themselves look better in this book that they talk to them.
paragraphs.cn: 整个处理过程令人作呕和恶心。而且，你知道，我曾在战情室参加过会议。迈克也是。那是一个进行国家安全对话的神圣场所。所以，他们讨论如何让唐纳德·特朗普摆脱爱泼斯坦文件的影响，这让我感到恶心，完全滥用了白宫的这些设施。但这本书中很多人只是想通过接受采访让自己看起来更好。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.en: So, the fact that they're even leaking on these meetings that were in the Situation Room is offensive to me.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，他们甚至泄露战情室会议内容的事实让我感到冒犯。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: So, I think the leaking of the meetings is the other thing I wanted to talk about with you. So, Mike, when you -- when you see that, right, and I should point out, CNN has reached out to both the White House and the DOJ, waiting to hear back. But the fact that there is so much detail in this book, so many quotes, what does that tell you about how this White House is working?
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我认为泄露会议内容是我想和你讨论的另一件事。迈克，当你看到这一点时 -- -- 我应该指出，CNN已经联系了白宫和司法部，正在等待回复。但书中如此多的细节、如此多的引语，这告诉你这个白宫是如何运作的？

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.en: Well, I'm not sure it tells you anything about how this White Bouse is working. And I think Meghan was spot on, this is a bunch of individuals who have left the administration who are trying to, you know, salvage their reputations. And that's what happens when you get leakers. I mean, there's really three leaks in Washington, one on public policy, you know, issues, one on going after somebody, your political enemy, and the other is to make yourself look better and more important.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我不确定这能告诉你这个白宫是如何运作的。我认为梅根说得对，这是一群已经离开政府的人，试图挽救他们的声誉。这就是泄密者出现的情况。华盛顿有三种泄密：一种是关于公共政策问题，一种是为了打击政治敌人，另一种是为了让自己看起来更好、更重要。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.en: And I think this is a classic example of that third instance of leakers talking to Swan and Haberman and that wonderful "New York Times" music that they play to make everything seem so serious about what they're revealing in their -- in their things. This is -- this is typical. The White House saw a crisis.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为这是第三种泄密的典型例子，泄密者向斯旺和哈伯曼透露信息，还有《纽约时报》那美妙的音乐，让一切看起来都很严肃。这很典型。白宫当时面临危机。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.en: They gathered the people together to give their advice to the president, made sure that everyone was on the -- on the same page, or at least aired out what they thought about this, and then went to the president with their decisions. I'm really not seeing anything here that is outstanding or unusual when it comes to the inner workings of a White House.
paragraphs.cn: 他们召集人员向总统提供建议，确保大家意见一致，或者至少表达了各自的想法，然后带着决定去见总统。我真的没有看到任何突出或不寻常的地方，就白宫内部运作而言。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: All right. Let's shift, if we could, to the group chat before I let you all go, let's take a break from the politics, people. Aaron, you kick this off because -- I can say kick and it relates directly to what's in your group chat.
paragraphs.cn: 好了，在我们结束之前，让我们转到群聊，暂时抛开政治。亚伦，你开始吧 -- -- 因为我说"开始"直接关联到你的群聊内容。

paragraphs.speaker: Blake
paragraphs.en: Yes, it's a big day. I mean the World Cup's starting. I've been excited about this for a long time. I would encourage people, and we've been talking about this with the people in my life, look at this guy Freddy from Germany. His Twitter feed about his travels through the American south. Experiencing true americana. It will -- it will resurrect your love for this country to see somebody experience these things for the first time.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，今天是个大日子。世界杯开始了。我期待已久。我鼓励大家，我们一直在和身边的人讨论，看看这个来自德国的弗雷迪。他的推特记录了他穿越美国南部的旅行。体验真正的美国文化。看到有人第一次体验这些事物，会重新点燃你对这个国家的热爱。

paragraphs.speaker: Blake
paragraphs.en: And it will change your perspective about these things that maybe you're starting to take for granted.
paragraphs.cn: 而且它会改变你对那些可能开始习以为常的事物的看法。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: You got to go to a Buc-ees that is for sure. Meghan, I'm going to go out on a limb here and I'm going to say "Summer House" in your group chat.
paragraphs.cn: 你一定要去一趟Buc-ees。梅根，我大胆预测一下，我要在你的群聊里说《夏日别墅》。

paragraphs.speaker: Hays
paragraphs.en: Of course. Of course. It's all that matters in my life is what's on Bravo. The third installment of "The Reunion" was this week. It was incredible. I think that there's a lot of -- a lot of thoughts back and forth of how people feel. The emotionlessness of Wes and Amanda was incredibly awkward for everyone involved. But, you know, I'm here for more in our new series of "In the City." I'm here for it.
paragraphs.cn: 当然，当然。我生命中最重要的就是Bravo电视台的节目。《重聚》第三集本周播出，太精彩了。我觉得大家对很多事情的看法反复不定。韦斯和阿曼达的冷漠让所有人都感到尴尬。但是，你知道，我期待我们新系列《在城市里》的更多内容。我准备好了。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: One of these days I'm actually going to watch it, I swear. Mike, you're talking dishwashers. The big debate, to rinse or
paragraphs.cn: 总有一天我会看的，我发誓。迈克，你在说洗碗机。大争论，是先冲洗还是

paragraphs.speaker: I -- Hill
paragraphs.en: No, you're not supposed to rinse.
paragraphs.cn: 不，你不应该冲洗。

paragraphs.speaker: Dubke
paragraphs.en: Yes, it's a -- like -- I -- you're not supposed to rinse. Modern dishwashers get the dishes clean. Found that out. But I love this Freddy story. The modern de Tocqueville, walking across the southeast of the United States, exposing what America is really like for Europe. I love it. I love it.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，这就像 -- -- 我 -- -- 你不应该冲洗。现代洗碗机能洗干净碗。我发现了这一点。但我喜欢弗雷迪的故事。现代的托克维尔，徒步穿越美国东南部，向欧洲展示美国的真实面貌。我喜欢，太喜欢了。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: All right, so you're joining in the group chat with Aaron. I love it when we bring people together. Good to see you guys.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，所以你加入了亚伦的群聊。我喜欢我们把人们聚在一起。很高兴见到你们。

paragraphs.speaker: Hill
paragraphs.en: Thanks for joining us. I'm Erica Hill. The headlines are next. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 感谢你的参与。我是埃里卡·希尔。接下来是头条新闻。

vocabulary.cn: 以牙还牙的，针锋相对的
vocabulary.en: characterized by retaliation in kind
vocabulary.excerpt: We do begin this hour with the tit-for-tat strikes between the U.S. and Iran
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我们首先关注美国和伊朗之间的针锋相对的打击。

vocabulary.en: close observation of a person or group, especially one under suspicion
vocabulary.excerpt: The U.S. says it targeted Iran's surveillance capabilities
vocabulary.exampleCn: 美国称其打击了伊朗的监视能力。

vocabulary.en: military actions and systems designed to protect against enemy air attacks
vocabulary.excerpt: communication systems and air defense sites
vocabulary.exampleCn: 通信系统和防空站点。

vocabulary.cn: 猛烈轰炸（expletive为脏话替代词）
vocabulary.en: to bomb heavily and destructively (with expletive for emphasis)
vocabulary.excerpt: he would bomb the expletive out of them
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他会猛烈轰炸他们。

vocabulary.en: a temporary suspension of fighting
vocabulary.excerpt: calling this the most violated ceasefire in history
vocabulary.exampleCn: 称这是历史上被违反最严重的停火。

vocabulary.en: to delay or string along repeatedly
vocabulary.excerpt: You can see when someone's trying to tap, tap, tap on a deal
vocabulary.exampleCn: 你能看到有人试图在协议上不断拖延。

vocabulary.cn: 用炸弹谈判（指以武力逼迫对方谈判）
vocabulary.en: to use military force as a means of coercion in negotiations
vocabulary.excerpt: If we need to negotiate with bombs, we'll negotiate with bombs
vocabulary.exampleCn: 如果我们需要用炸弹谈判，我们就用炸弹谈判。

vocabulary.en: the act of frightening someone to make them do something
vocabulary.excerpt: No sustainable deal can be reached through threats, intimidation, or the use of force
vocabulary.exampleCn: 通过威胁、恐吓或使用武力无法达成可持续的协议。

vocabulary.en: to stop oneself from doing something
vocabulary.excerpt: The president of the United States should refrain from his recurring threats against Iran
vocabulary.exampleCn: 美国总统应避免对伊朗反复发出威胁。

vocabulary.en: the practice of persuading someone to do something by using force or threats
vocabulary.excerpt: Iran has never negotiated under threats and pressure and will never submit to pressure or coercion
vocabulary.exampleCn: 伊朗从未在威胁和压力下谈判，也永远不会屈服于压力或胁迫。

vocabulary.en: an act of sealing off a place to prevent goods or people from entering or leaving
vocabulary.excerpt: Then they tried the blockade for another two months
vocabulary.exampleCn: 然后他们又尝试封锁了两个月。

vocabulary.en: an act carried out during an armed conflict that violates international laws of war
vocabulary.excerpt: The other really worrying escalation we saw yesterday was an apparent war crime
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我们昨天看到的另一个真正令人担忧的升级是明显的战争罪。

vocabulary.en: a military attack with high accuracy, often using guided munitions
vocabulary.excerpt: a U.S. targeted precision strike on a water reservoir for drinking water in Iran near Hormuz
vocabulary.exampleCn: 美国对伊朗霍尔木兹附近的一个饮用水水库进行了精确打击。

vocabulary.en: the basic physical systems and structures that serve the civilian population
vocabulary.excerpt: targeting of civilian infrastructure, as a deliberate strategy
vocabulary.exampleCn: 将民用基础设施作为目标，作为一种蓄意策略。

vocabulary.en: behavior that is intended to impress or mislead others
vocabulary.excerpt: There is so much public posturing from this president
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这位总统公开做了很多故作姿态的表演。

vocabulary.en: to weaken or damage gradually or insidiously
vocabulary.excerpt: has only further undermined that trust
vocabulary.exampleCn: 只会进一步削弱那种信任。

vocabulary.en: to disclose secret information
vocabulary.excerpt: Trump comes out and leaks some of the details
vocabulary.exampleCn: 特朗普出来泄露了一些细节。

vocabulary.en: the person or group with whom one is negotiating
vocabulary.excerpt: earn the trust of his negotiating counterpart
vocabulary.exampleCn: 赢得谈判对手的信任。

vocabulary.en: with honest intentions
vocabulary.excerpt: convince Trump to negotiate in good faith
vocabulary.exampleCn: 说服特朗普真诚地谈判。

vocabulary.en: immediately after
vocabulary.excerpt: coming on the heels of Iran and Israel trading strikes earlier in the week
vocabulary.exampleCn: 紧随本周早些时候伊朗和以色列互相打击之后。

vocabulary.en: to be in charge; to make the decisions
vocabulary.excerpt: Israel doesn't call the shots
vocabulary.exampleCn: 以色列不能发号施令。

vocabulary.en: influence or power over someone
vocabulary.excerpt: how much sway, how much influence do you think this president actually has
vocabulary.exampleCn: 你认为这位总统实际上有多大影响力？

vocabulary.en: the power to influence a person or situation
vocabulary.excerpt: President Trump and any U.S. president has nearly infinite leverage over the Israeli government
vocabulary.exampleCn: 特朗普总统和任何美国总统对以色列政府几乎拥有无限的影响力。

vocabulary.en: to discourage someone from doing something
vocabulary.excerpt: it's deterred Netanyahu from launching what potentially started this escalation
vocabulary.exampleCn: 它阻止了内塔尼亚胡发动可能引发升级的行动。

sentences.en: New overnight, the United States and Iran trading attacks for a second straight day as President Trump promises more to come if Tehran does not immediately agree to a peace deal.
sentences.cn: 最新消息，美国与伊朗连续第二天互相攻击，特朗普总统承诺，如果德黑兰不立即同意和平协议，还会有更多打击。
sentences.structure: 主句 + as引导的时间状语从句，从句中包含if引导的条件状语从句
sentences.analysis: trading attacks 是新闻压缩表达，意为'互相攻击'；for a second straight day 表示'连续第二天'；more to come 是省略结构，完整为 more attacks to come

sentences.en: Plus, new revelations about how the White House handled the Epstein scandal, meeting in the Situation Room to game out a plan without President Trump.
sentences.cn: 此外，关于白宫如何处理爱泼斯坦丑闻的新爆料：他们在战情室开会，在没有特朗普总统的情况下制定计划。
sentences.structure: 主句 + how引导的宾语从句，后接现在分词短语作伴随状语
sentences.analysis: revelations 意为'爆料'；game out 是动词短语，意为'制定策略'；Situation Room 是白宫战情室

sentences.en: The president suggests, as you just heard, he doesn't mind the high prices right now.
sentences.cn: 正如你刚才听到的，总统暗示他并不介意目前的高物价。
sentences.structure: 主句 + as引导的非限制性定语从句，后接省略that的宾语从句
sentences.analysis: as you just heard 是插入语，as 指代后面整个句子；suggests 在此意为'暗示'

sentences.en: Could we also see a much-needed vibe shift away from the outrage over ticket prices and immigration issues?
sentences.cn: 我们是否也能看到一种急需的氛围转变，摆脱对票价和移民问题的愤怒？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句，主语we，谓语see，宾语a much-needed vibe shift，后接away from短语作定语
sentences.analysis: vibe shift 是口语化表达，意为'氛围转变'；much-needed 是复合形容词，意为'急需的'；outrage over 意为'对……的愤怒'

sentences.en: President Trump choosing military might over diplomacy.
sentences.cn: 特朗普总统选择军事力量而非外交手段。
sentences.structure: 独立主格结构，现在分词短语作伴随状语
sentences.analysis: choosing...over... 是固定搭配，意为'选择……而不是……'；military might 意为'军事力量'

sentences.en: On a call from the Situation Room, President Trump told FOX News if Iran did not agree to a deal, he would bomb the expletive out of them, calling this the most violated ceasefire in history.
sentences.cn: 在战情室的一次通话中，特朗普总统告诉福克斯新闻，如果伊朗不同意协议，他就会狠狠地轰炸他们，并称这是历史上最被违反的停火协议。
sentences.structure: 介词短语作状语 + 主句 + if引导的条件状语从句 + 现在分词短语作伴随状语
sentences.analysis: the expletive 是委婉语，指脏话，此处表示强调；calling this... 是现在分词短语，this 指代整个情况

sentences.en: Instead, they're going to have tap, tap, tap bombs dropping on key facilities in Iran from the United States of America.
sentences.cn: 相反，他们将会有来自美国的炸弹不断落在伊朗的关键设施上。
sentences.structure: 主句，have + 宾语 + 现在分词作宾补
sentences.analysis: tap, tap, tap 是拟声词，模拟连续打击，此处与前面的'tap, tap, tap on a deal'形成对比；have bombs dropping 是使役结构

sentences.en: No sustainable deal can be reached through threats, intimidation, or the use of force.
sentences.cn: 通过威胁、恐吓或使用武力无法达成任何可持续的协议。
sentences.structure: 简单句，主语 + 谓语 + 介词短语作方式状语
sentences.analysis: sustainable deal 意为'可持续的协议'；through 表示方式；threats, intimidation, the use of force 是并列名词短语

sentences.en: The president of the United States should refrain from his recurring threats against Iran, including his renewed threat of force made today.
sentences.cn: 美国总统应避免对伊朗一再发出威胁，包括今天再次发出的武力威胁。
sentences.structure: 主句 + 现在分词短语including...作补充说明，其中made today是过去分词短语作定语
sentences.analysis: refrain from 意为'避免'；recurring threats 意为'反复的威胁'；renewed threat 意为'再次发出的威胁'

sentences.en: Iran has never negotiated under threats and pressure and will never submit to pressure or coercion.
sentences.cn: 伊朗从未在威胁和压力下谈判，也永远不会屈服于压力或胁迫。
sentences.structure: 并列句，由and连接两个谓语部分
sentences.analysis: under threats and pressure 是介词短语作状语；submit to 意为'屈服于'；coercion 意为'胁迫'

sentences.en: We know for certain from recent history that it is not the way to get Iran to negotiate.
sentences.cn: 我们从近期历史中确切知道，这不是让伊朗谈判的方式。
sentences.structure: 主句 + that引导的宾语从句
sentences.analysis: for certain 是固定短语，意为'确切地'；get sb. to do sth. 意为'让某人做某事'

sentences.en: They tried bombing Iran for 40 days.
sentences.cn: 他们曾尝试轰炸伊朗40天。
sentences.structure: 简单句，try + 动名词作宾语
sentences.analysis: try doing sth. 意为'尝试做某事'；for 40 days 是时间状语

sentences.en: It's unfortunate, because I think there is still a possibility for peace when the Trump administration gets serious about actually negotiating, which is going to require them making some concessions.
sentences.cn: 这很不幸，因为我认为当特朗普政府认真对待实际谈判时，和平仍有可能，而这将要求他们做出一些让步。
sentences.structure: 主句 + because引导的原因状语从句，从句中包含think的宾语从句，宾语从句中包含when引导的时间状语从句和which引导的非限制性定语从句
sentences.analysis: gets serious about 意为'认真对待'；require sb. doing sth. 是require的用法，意为'要求某人做某事'；concessions 意为'让步'

sentences.en: The other really worrying escalation we saw yesterday was an apparent war crime: a U.S. targeted precision strike on a water reservoir for drinking water in Iran near Hormuz.
sentences.cn: 我们昨天看到的另一个真正令人担忧的升级是明显的战争罪行：美国对伊朗霍尔木兹附近的一个饮用水水库进行了精准打击。
sentences.structure: 主句（含省略that的定语从句we saw yesterday）+ 同位语（冒号后解释war crime）
sentences.analysis: escalation 意为'升级'；apparent war crime 意为'明显的战争罪行'；targeted precision strike 意为'精准打击'
